description: Project scaffold and base structure: Set up the foundational project structure with all necessary configuration files, entry points, and folder organization so that all subsequent features have a stable base to build on.
acceptance_criteria: Project directory structure exists with clearly separated concerns (markup, styles, logic, assets)
A local development environment can be started and serves a blank page without errors
A README.md exists describing how to run the project locally

description: City search input: Provide a text input field where the user can type a city name and submit it to trigger a weather lookup.
acceptance_criteria: A visible text input is present on the page with a placeholder indicating 'Enter city name'
A submit mechanism (button or Enter key) is available to trigger the search
Submitting with an empty input does not trigger a lookup and shows a validation message
The input is accessible (proper label, keyboard navigable)

description: Weather API integration: Connect to a public weather API to retrieve current weather data for a given city name, returning temperature, weather description, and icon identifier.
acceptance_criteria: A request to the weather API with a valid city name returns structured data containing temperature, description, and icon info
The API key or access method is configurable and not hardcoded in source
Network errors are caught and do not crash the application

description: Temperature display: Show the current temperature for the searched city in a clear, prominent format after a successful API response.
acceptance_criteria: Temperature value is displayed on screen after a successful city search
Temperature includes a unit indicator (e.g., °C or °F)
Temperature is not displayed before the first search or during loading

description: Weather description display: Show a human-readable weather description (e.g., 'clear sky', 'partly cloudy') for the searched city.
acceptance_criteria: A textual weather description is shown alongside the temperature after a successful search
The description comes from the API response, not from a static mapping
The description is hidden before any search is performed

description: Weather condition icon: Display a visual icon representing the current weather condition (e.g., sun, clouds, rain) alongside the weather data.
acceptance_criteria: An icon or image is displayed that visually represents the current weather condition
The icon changes appropriately for different weather conditions (e.g., sunny vs rainy)
The icon has an alt text or accessible label describing the condition
No broken image is shown if the icon fails to load

description: Error handling for invalid city: Provide clear user feedback when a city name is not found or the API returns an error.
acceptance_criteria: Searching for a non-existent city displays a user-friendly error message (e.g., 'City not found')
The error message is visually distinct from normal weather results
Previous weather results are cleared or replaced when an error occurs
The user can immediately try a new search after an error

description: Loading state indicator: Show a visual loading indicator while the weather data is being fetched so the user knows the request is in progress.
acceptance_criteria: A loading indicator (spinner, text, or animation) is visible while the API request is in flight
The loading indicator disappears once data is received or an error occurs
The submit button or input is disabled or visually indicates 'busy' during loading

description: Responsive and polished UI layout: Ensure the application looks clean and usable on both desktop and mobile screen sizes.
acceptance_criteria: The layout adapts gracefully to screen widths from 320px to 1440px
Text is legible, inputs are tappable on mobile, and spacing is consistent
The weather result area is visually grouped and easy to scan
The overall appearance is clean and professional (no unstyled HTML)
